2605.12620.title: Think Twice, Act Once: Verifier-Guided Action Selection For Embodied Agents
2605.12620.paper_summary: Building generalist embodied agents capable of solving complex real-world tasks remains a fundamental challenge in AI. Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of such agents through strong vision-language knowledge and chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, yet remain brittle when faced with challenging out-of-distribution scenarios. To address this, we propose Verifier-Guided Action Selection (VegAS), a test-time framework designed to improve the robustness of MLLM-based embodied agents through an explicit verification step. At inference time, rather than committing to a single decoded action, VeGAS samples an ensemble of candidate actions and uses a generative verifier to identify the most reliable choice, without modifying the underlying policy. Crucially, we find that using an MLLM off-the-shelf as a verifier yields no improvement, motivating our LLM-driven data synthesis strategy, which automatically constructs a diverse curriculum of failure cases to expose the verifier to a rich distribution of potential errors at training time. Across embodied reasoning benchmarks spanning the Habitat and ALFRED environments, VeGAS consistently improves generalization, achieving up to a 36% relative performance gain over strong CoT baselines on the most challenging multi-object, long-horizon tasks.
2605.12620.paper_summary_zh: 建立能夠解決複雜現實世界任務的通用具身代理仍然是人工智慧中的一項基本挑戰。多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）透過強大的視覺-語言知識和思維鏈（CoT）推理，顯著提升了這類代理的推理能力，但在面對具有挑戰性的分佈外場景時仍然顯得脆弱。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了驗證者引導的行動選擇（VegAS），這是一個旨在通過明確的驗證步驟提高基於MLLM的具身代理穩健性的測試時框架。在推理時，VegAS不是承諾於單一解碼行動，而是從候選行動的集合中進行抽樣，並使用生成驗證者來識別最可靠的選擇，而不修改基礎策略。關鍵是，我們發現使用現成的MLLM作為驗證者並未帶來改善，這促使我們的LLM驅動數據合成策略，自動構建多樣化的失敗案例課程，以便在訓練時將驗證者暴露於豐富的潛在錯誤分佈中。在涵蓋Habitat和ALFRED環境的具身推理基準中，VegAS始終提高了泛化能力，在最具挑戰性的多物體、長期任務上實現了相對於強CoT基準高達36%的性能增益。
2605.12620.author: Nishad Singhi et.al.
2605.12620.authors: Nishad Singhi, Christian Bialas, Snehal Jauhri, Vignesh Prasad, Georgia Chalvatzaki, Marcus Rohrbach, Anna Rohrbach
